body:
Hi Phil, just checking in before the linen order goes out this week. Do you have the towel and sheet counts from the back rooms, or should I walk through and count them myself?

Also, are we sticking with Green Mountain Linen like last season, or did Elaine want to try the new supplier? Let me know if it's easier to sort at the front desk.

Nancy
snippet: Hi Phil, just checking in before the linen order goes out this week. Do you h...
subject: Front desk supply order

body:
Hi Nancy, good timing — I'll have the towel and sheet counts to you by Thursday, no need to walk through yourself. Let's stay with Green Mountain Linen for now, but I'd like to add an extra case of the bath sheets since we ran short over the holiday weekend.

I think that covers it without needing to meet. Let me know if anything else comes up before the order goes in.

Phil
snippet: Hi Nancy, good timing — I'll have the towel and sheet counts to you by Thursd...
subject: Re: Front desk supply order

body:
Phil, I've started sketching out the summer front-desk setup and wanted your input before I run it by Elaine. Right now I'm thinking: fresh brochures in the morning, the foliage slideshow on the lobby screen, then the local-events board updated each Friday. Does that flow make sense to you?

Still deciding where to put the new coffee station — a couple of spots in the lobby but open to ideas. What worked well last summer?

Nancy
snippet: Phil, I've started sketching out the summer front-desk setup and wanted your ...
subject: Summer front desk setup

body:
Nancy, the plan looks good overall. One suggestion — I'd put the coffee station near the window by the reading chairs; guests gravitate there in the morning and it kept the desk line short last year.

For the events board, let's make sure we leave room for the Latchis and Music Center flyers — guests ask about those constantly. Last summer it felt a bit crowded by August, so a little breathing room helps.

Phil
snippet: Nancy, the plan looks good overall. One suggestion — I'd put the coffee stati...
subject: Re: Summer front desk setup

body:
Good call on the window spot — makes a lot of sense. I'll move the coffee station there today and check we have enough outlets.

I'll keep a clear column on the board for the Latchis and Music Center flyers. Planning to have a working layout ready by Friday so you and Elaine can look it over the weekend. I'll flag your notes at the top.

Nancy
snippet: Good call on the window spot — makes a lot of sense. I'll move the coffee sta...
subject: Re: Summer front desk setup

body:
Hi Phil, now that Amy's finished her front-desk training, I'm putting together the weekend coverage plan before our Monday staff meeting. Can you note your Saturday availability if you haven't already? The sheet is in the back office.

My read is that pairing Amy with Hector on Saturdays works best, but I'd rather hear your take before I lock it in.

Nancy
snippet: Hi Phil, now that Amy's finished her front-desk training, I'm putting togethe...
subject: Weekend coverage plan

body:
I filled in my Saturdays this morning. Short version: I can take the early shift two Saturdays a month, the ones I'm not driving to Greenfield for lunch with Pat. Pairing Amy with Hector is a good idea — he's patient with the booking system and she's still learning it.

Are you planning to post the schedule before the meeting or work through it together Monday?

Nancy, let me know.

Phil
snippet: I filled in my Saturdays this morning. Short version: I can take the early sh...
subject: Re: Weekend coverage plan

body:
That matches my thinking too. I'll put together a clean schedule with everyone's hours and post it Sunday evening so the team comes to the meeting ready — shouldn't be more than a page.

One thing worth flagging: the third weekend in July is the wedding block, so we'll need extra hands at check-in. Good to plan for it now.

Nancy
snippet: That matches my thinking too. I'll put together a clean schedule with everyon...
subject: Re: Weekend coverage plan

body:
Posting it ahead of time is the right call. On the wedding weekend — since it'll be busy, I'd suggest we keep that as an open discussion Monday rather than trying to settle it on the sheet. Easier to sort out who's free in person.

I can help cover the front desk if it gets hectic. See you Monday.

Phil
snippet: Posting it ahead of time is the right call. On the wedding weekend — since it...
subject: Re: Weekend coverage plan

body:
Phil, I'd like to go over the front-desk petty cash and the summer supply spend before we close out the month. Can you pull together a short summary of what we've spent at the desk so far, with a note on anything that ran high? I'll need it a day before we meet so I have time to look it over.

Are you available next Tuesday or Wednesday afternoon?

Elaine
snippet: Phil, I'd like to go over the front-desk petty cash and the summer supply spe...
subject: Petty cash and season prep

body:
Hi Elaine, I can have the summary ready by Monday end of day. I can already flag that the coffee and pastry line ran a bit high this month — we had the extra guests over the holiday weekend, so I'll explain that in the note.

Tuesday afternoon works well for me. Would 2pm work on your end?

Phil
snippet: Hi Elaine, I can have the summary ready by Monday end of day. I can already f...
subject: Re: Petty cash and season prep

body:
Tuesday at 2pm works. Please also give me a quick look ahead — I want to know whether the higher coffee spend is just the holiday weekend or something we should plan for through the summer. If you've already adjusted the standing order, note that too.

Also, please loop Nancy in since she handles the supplier orders.

Elaine
snippet: Tuesday at 2pm works. Please also give me a quick look ahead — I want to know...
subject: Re: Petty cash and season prep

body:
Will do. I'll add a short look-ahead for the summer and note what I've already changed — I trimmed the standing pastry order back to weekdays, which should even things out by July. I'll copy Nancy on the summary.

Is there anything else you'd like me to break out, or any part you want laid out differently?

Phil
snippet: Will do. I'll add a short look-ahead for the summer and note what I've alread...
subject: Re: Petty cash and season prep

body:
That covers it for now. One more thing — please keep the guest-amenity spend separate from the office supplies. The bookkeeper has been asking for that split and I'd like it clean before the quarterly check-in.

Thanks for turning this around quickly, Phil. See you Tuesday.

Elaine
snippet: That covers it for now. One more thing — please keep the guest-amenity spend ...
subject: Re: Petty cash and season prep

body:
Hi Mom, are you free for lunch any day this week? I'm coming down from Burlington Thursday and thought we could try that new place on Main Street you mentioned. No pressure at all if the inn is busy — just figured I'd check.

Colleen
snippet: Hi Mom, are you free for lunch any day this week? I'm coming down from Burlin...
subject: Lunch this week?

body:
Thursday works great — I'll make it happen. I've been wanting to try that place since it opened. How does noon sound? We could walk over from the inn if the weather holds up.

Mom
snippet: Thursday works great — I'll make it happen. I've been wanting to try that pla...
subject: Re: Lunch this week?

body:
Mom, are you around next Tuesday morning? I have an appointment in Brattleboro and thought maybe we could grab coffee after — nothing fancy, just a chance to catch up properly. It's been a few weeks since we've had any real time to talk.

Let me know what works for you.

Colleen
snippet: Mom, are you around next Tuesday morning? I have an appointment in Brattlebor...
subject: Coffee next Tuesday?

body:
Tuesday works perfectly. I'm off until my afternoon shift, so the morning is open. How about Mocha Joe's at 9? That gives you plenty of buffer before anything else. Looking forward to it — you're right, it's been too long.

Mom
snippet: Tuesday works perfectly. I'm off until my afternoon shift, so the morning is ...
subject: Re: Coffee next Tuesday?

body:
Perfect, 9 at Mocha Joe's it is! I'll see you there. Fair warning — Zoë and Eli have about a thousand things they want me to tell you, so come prepared.

Colleen
snippet: Perfect, 9 at Mocha Joe's it is! I'll see you there. Fair warning — Zoë and E...
subject: Re: Coffee next Tuesday?

body:
Mom! Your birthday is coming up and I really want to do something special this year. I was thinking a brunch at the house — nothing too big, just close family. Maybe Sunday the 13th? I can cook or we can do a spread, whatever you'd prefer. Just say the word.

Colleen
snippet: Mom! Your birthday is coming up and I really want to do something special thi...
subject: Birthday brunch plans

body:
That sounds really lovely, Colleen. Sunday the 13th works perfectly. I'd love a home-cooked brunch — honestly your frittata alone is reason enough to celebrate. Just keep it small and relaxed, that's all I ask. No fuss.

Mom
snippet: That sounds really lovely, Colleen. Sunday the 13th works perfectly. I'd love...
subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans

body:
Small and relaxed, I promise! I'll do the frittata, some pastries, and the kids want to help bake a cake. I'm thinking 11am so it doesn't run too late into the afternoon.

I'll call Uncle Brian and Aunt Pat — is there anyone else you'd want there?

Colleen
snippet: Small and relaxed, I promise! I'll do the frittata, some pastries, and the ki...
subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans

body:
11 sounds perfect. Yes to Brian and Pat — and if it's not too much, maybe Rev. Whitaker and his wife? They've been so kind this past year and it would be nice to have them. Can't wait. Thank you for putting this together, and tell Zoë and Eli I can't wait to see them.

Mom
snippet: 11 sounds perfect. Yes to Brian and Pat — and if it's not too much, maybe Rev...
subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans

body:
Hi Mom, just a heads-up — the streaming plan I share with you is coming up for renewal next month and the price went up again. It's now $22.99 a month, which feels like a lot. I wasn't sure if you wanted to keep it as-is or look at a cheaper plan. Thought I'd flag it before it auto-renews.

Colleen
snippet: Hi Mom, just a heads-up — the streaming plan I share with you is coming up fo...
subject: Streaming renewal options

body:
Thanks for catching that. Honestly I only watch the British dramas and the baking shows on the iPad after dinner — not much else. But if you and the kids use it I don't mind keeping it. Is there a cheaper tier? I'd be fine with it as long as the same shows are there.

Mom
snippet: Thanks for catching that. Honestly I only watch the British dramas and the ba...
subject: Re: Streaming renewal options

body:
Yes, the ad-supported plan is $7.99 a month, which is way more reasonable. The shows are basically the same, you just get a few ads. For how often you watch I really think that's the right call. Want me to go ahead and switch it over?

Colleen
snippet: Yes, the ad-supported plan is $7.99 a month, which is way more reasonable. Th...
subject: Re: Streaming renewal options

body:
Go ahead and switch it — sounds like a no-brainer. It's all on your account anyway, so whatever's easiest for you. Let me know if anything comes up with the billing, otherwise just take care of it. Thanks for handling this.

Mom
snippet: Go ahead and switch it — sounds like a no-brainer. It's all on your account a...
subject: Re: Streaming renewal options

body:
Done! I switched it over and the new rate kicks in at the next billing cycle. I also reset the iPad login since the old one kept logging you out — I'll text you the new one so it's saved somewhere.

All sorted!

Colleen
snippet: Done! I switched it over and the new rate kicks in at the next billing cycle....
subject: Re: Streaming renewal options

body:
Hi Mom, I got an invoice from Sam Pereira addressed to you but sent to my email — no idea how the mix-up happened. It's for $340 and dated last week. Did you have him out recently, or do you think this might be a billing error? I can forward you the PDF if you want to take a look.

Colleen
snippet: Hi Mom, I got an invoice from Sam Pereira addressed to you but sent to my ema...
subject: Handyman invoice question

body:
Yes, please forward it. Sam did come out to patch the basement window and check the porch railing, but $340 is higher than what we talked about when I booked him. I'll give him a call and get it sorted out.

Sorry you got roped into my billing mess — thanks for flagging it instead of just ignoring it.

Mom
snippet: Yes, please forward it. Sam did come out to patch the basement window and che...
subject: Re: Handyman invoice question

body:
Mom, my lease in Burlington is up at the end of August and my landlord just sent over the renewal options. Option A is a 12-month renewal with a 6% rent increase, and Option B is month-to-month at a higher base rate. I'm trying to figure out which makes more sense. Would you have time this week to talk it through?

Colleen
snippet: Mom, my lease in Burlington is up at the end of August and my landlord just s...
subject: Apartment lease renewal

body:
Of course — happy to think it through with you. On first read, if you and the kids are planning to stay put through next summer, the 12-month renewal almost certainly makes more sense even with the increase. Month-to-month only really pays off if you're expecting a big change soon.

I'm free Wednesday evening or anytime this weekend. What works for you?

Mom
snippet: Of course — happy to think it through with you. On first read, if you and the...
subject: Re: Apartment lease renewal

body:
Wednesday evening works well. And you're probably right — we don't have any big moves planned, so locking in the 12-month is likely the smarter call. I'll bring the full lease so we can read through it properly instead of just going off the summary.

Thanks, Mom. See you Wednesday.

Colleen
snippet: Wednesday evening works well. And you're probably right — we don't have any b...
subject: Re: Apartment lease renewal

body:
Hi Mom, I finished that new Louise Penny mystery and thought of you the whole way through — it's exactly your kind of cozy. I put it on hold for you at Brooks Memorial so you can grab it on your Thursday return run. There's also a quilting book in the same series of holds.

Colleen
snippet: Hi Mom, I finished that new Louise Penny mystery and thought of you the whole...
subject: This week's reading

body:
Oh wonderful — I've been waiting for the new Louise Penny. I'll pick it up Thursday when I drop off the others. The quilting book sounds perfect too; I've been looking for new block patterns for the fall guild meeting. Thank you for putting them on hold for me.

Mom
snippet: Oh wonderful — I've been waiting for the new Louise Penny. I'll pick it up Th...
subject: Re: This week's reading

body:
You'll love it. It's the one set in the village in winter, which is the cozy kind you like best. The hold should be ready by Wednesday, so Thursday timing is perfect.

There was also a corn chowder recipe in the back of one of the cooking magazines I think you'd like — I'll snap a photo and send it along.

Colleen
snippet: You'll love it. It's the one set in the village in winter, which is the cozy ...
subject: Re: This week's reading

body:
Picked up both this morning — the library had them ready right at the desk. Send the chowder recipe when you get a chance; it's about time I made a big pot before the weather turns. Good recommendations, as always.

Mom
snippet: Picked up both this morning — the library had them ready right at the desk. S...
subject: Re: This week's reading

body:
Hi Mom, I wanted to check about next week. Eli has soccer Tuesday that runs until 5, and Zoë has her art class Wednesday afternoon. Any chance you could come up and help with pickup Tuesday? That would be a huge help, and you know Eli loves when Grandma comes to practice.

Colleen
snippet: Hi Mom, I wanted to check about next week. Eli has soccer Tuesday that runs u...
subject: Helping with the kids next week

body:
Tuesday works for me. Should I be at the school around 3:15 like usual? I can take them for a snack and then get Eli to practice — happy to wait or come back, whatever's easier for you.

Mom
snippet: Tuesday works for me. Should I be at the school around 3:15 like usual? I can...
subject: Re: Helping with the kids next week

body:
3:15 is perfect, thank you so much. A snack and then practice would be ideal — Eli's carpool has been unreliable lately and this really takes the stress off. I'll let the school know you're on the pickup list for Tuesday.

Any chance you could also do Wednesday? I have a long day at work and I hate the idea of scrambling for Zoë's class.

Colleen
snippet: 3:15 is perfect, thank you so much. A snack and then practice would be ideal ...
subject: Re: Helping with the kids next week

body:
Wednesday is fine too — I know where the art class is, I'll take her. Don't go finding other coverage, it's not too much at all. You focus on your day and I'll have both days handled.

Mom
snippet: Wednesday is fine too — I know where the art class is, I'll take her. Don't g...
subject: Re: Helping with the kids next week

body:
You are an absolute lifesaver, I hope you know that. I'll pack their bags the night before and leave a note with everything you need. I seriously owe you dinner for this — that little Italian place you like, on me.

Thank you, Mom. Really.

Colleen
snippet: You are an absolute lifesaver, I hope you know that. I'll pack their bags the...
subject: Re: Helping with the kids next week

body:
Mom, now that we've settled on the last week of August up here, I wanted to start locking down the details. I found the same cottage on the lake we liked last year — it sleeps eight and has the deck right on the water. It's available and right in the budget we talked about. Should I go ahead and put a hold on it?

These places book up fast in August so I'd want to move quickly if you're on board.

Colleen
snippet: Mom, now that we've settled on the last week of August up here, I wanted to s...
subject: August cottage week

body:
Yes, go ahead and hold it — sounds perfect. Send me the link and the deposit amount and I'll send my share by the end of the week. A deck on the water with the kids is exactly what I was picturing. I'll put in for the PTO week with Elaine right away. Can't wait.

Mom
snippet: Yes, go ahead and hold it — sounds perfect. Send me the link and the deposit ...
subject: Re: August cottage week

body:
Hi Mom, do you have anything going on this weekend? The forecast looks really nice for Saturday — I was thinking about bringing the kids down to the Brattleboro farmers market in the morning and then maybe a walk along the river after. Would you want to join us? Totally low-key, no agenda.

Colleen
snippet: Hi Mom, do you have anything going on this weekend? The forecast looks really...
subject: Weekend plans

body:
That sounds like a great Saturday — count me in. I'll meet you at the market. What time are you thinking? If we get there early enough I can grab some of that sourdough from the bakery stall before it sells out.

Mom
snippet: That sounds like a great Saturday — count me in. I'll meet you at the market....
subject: Re: Weekend plans

body:
How about 9am? That should be early enough for the sourdough and the good produce stalls. The kids will be so excited — Zoë has been asking when she gets to see Grandma again. See you Saturday morning!

Colleen
snippet: How about 9am? That should be early enough for the sourdough and the good pro...
subject: Re: Weekend plans
